claim: Build agents with two core CLI tools (steer for GUI control, drive for terminal control via tmux) instead of dangerous omnipotent OpenClaw-style agents
actionable: Equip agents with GUI control (steer) and terminal automation (drive via tmux) through dedicated CLI tools rather than code generation
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw

claim: Deploy agents on dedicated hardware (Mac mini, Mac Air, Mac Pro) rather than in containers to give them full device autonomy
actionable: Set up a Mac device as a dedicated agent environment with a listen HTTP server, allowing agents to fully control that device
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw

claim: Use YAML job system for scaling to multiple agents across multiple devices with a trigger layer (HTTP server) and job management
actionable: Define jobs in YAML, use HTTP servers to trigger agent execution, and track job status via command-line tools
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw

claim: Employ 'just' file as a quick command runner to abstract away complex CLI invocations and template agent workflows
actionable: Use just file for parameterized command shortcuts; call just commands inside just commands to create reusable workflow templates
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw

claim: Define lightweight skills with 130-line documentation on how agents should use device tools (focus, verify, observe loop pattern)
actionable: Write concise skill documentation with clear observation loops and screen awareness rules for each tool
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw

claim: Security risk with OpenClaw-style agents: they generate vulnerable code at scale and are susceptible to prompt injection attacks
actionable: Avoid unrestricted code generation; use bounded agent tools with explicit capabilities instead
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw

claim: When agents increase autonomy, operator autonomy also increases—giving agents device control unlocks new engineering workflows
actionable: Design agent systems assuming humans will delegate entire task pipelines; focus on observability and trust-building
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw

claim: Agentic engineering is knowing what agents are doing so well you don't have to look; vibe coding is not knowing and not looking
actionable: Build observable systems; understand your agents' behavior through logs, job tracking, and proof-of-work artifacts
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw

claim: Use AirDrop for agent result delivery on macOS—creates a clean notification workflow for async task completion
actionable: Have agents write outputs to temp directory and use AirDrop to deliver results back to user device
source_title: Mac Mini Agents: Use Skills Instead of OpenClaw
